title: Developing BM TME models with holistic, multimodal AI-driven analysis
description: The purpose of this aim is to use our advanced single cell technologies to study the immune activity in the BM TME and identify targets for antibodies and cytokines to modulate the immune activity in the brain to more anti-tumor activity.

research_tasks.title: Temporal understanding of immune activity in BM TME
research_tasks.description: Research immune temporal changes using Zman-seq in the BM TME using our previous research adapting it from glioma to BM.

research_tasks.title: Immune cell-cell interaction in the BM TME
research_tasks.description: Use PIC-seq to measure immune cell interaction in the BM TME.
relationships: Task 1.2's cell interaction data complements Task 1.3's spatial data for a comprehensive understanding in objective 1.

research_tasks.title: Immune spatial distribution in the BM TME
research_tasks.description: Use stereo-seq to study spatial distribution of immune cells in the BM TME.
relationships: Task 1.3's spatial data complements Task 1.2's interaction data to establish a comprehensive understanding of BM TME.
Objective 1 provides foundational data on the BM TME, informing cytokine selection in Objective 2 and immunocytokine design in Objective 3.

title: Preclinical screening of cytokines in orthotopic immunocompetent BM models
description: The purpose of this aim is to use our advanced single cell technologies to study the immune activity in the BM TME and identify targets for antibodies and cytokines to modulate the immune activity in the brain to more anti-tumor activity.

research_tasks.title: Screening of cytokines in BM TME
research_tasks.description: Use our in-house cytokine library to screen for cytokines that modulate immune activity in the BM TME.

research_tasks.title: In-vitro validation of cytokines
research_tasks.description: Use in-vitro models to validate the cytokines identified in task 1.
relationships: Task 2.2 uses in-vitro models to validate the cytokine hits identified in Task 2.1.

research_tasks.title: In-vivo validation of cytokines
research_tasks.description: Single-cell analysis using in-vitro and in vivo functional screening system on myeloid, NK and T cell activity for trans-acting MiTEs.
relationships: Task 2.3 uses in-vivo functional screening to validate cytokines identified in task 2.1 and validated in task 2.2.
Objective 2 screens cytokines, which are then used in the design of immunocytokines in Objective 3.

title: Design of tumor-targeting immunocytokines
description: The purpose of this aim is to use our advanced single cell technologies to study the immune activity in the BM TME and identify targets for antibodies and cytokines to modulate the immune activity in the brain to more anti-tumor activity.

research_tasks.title: Design fusion proteins and cleavage site
research_tasks.description: We will develop the optimal structures for the fusion proteins of the top 3-5 mAb-cytokine combinations identified in Aim 2, using advanced techniques in protein design. The design process will include the selection of the most suitable peptide linkers, blocking moieties, TAM-specific cleavage sites and the computational optimization of protein structure and stability.

research_tasks.title: Produce fusion proteins
research_tasks.description: We will manufacture the 3-5 mAb-cytokine fusion proteins. The protein synthesis will be done by a contract research organization (CRO) selected based on our experience with leading CROs based on quality and punctual production. The production will include the selection of stable molecules with high protein expression and no aggregation.
relationships: Task 3.2 is dependent on the protein design output from Task 3.1.

research_tasks.title: In-vitro validation of immunocytokines
research_tasks.description: We will confirm the binding via SPR, ELISA, cell-based binding and reporter assays. We will validate immunocytokines' impact on interactions between myeloid and lymphoid cell activity using in-vitro assays of co-cultured huMDMs, NK or T cells. To assess the efficacy of the fusion proteins in inducing cytotoxic NK and T cell activity, assays of co-cultured huMDMs, NK, or T cells and tumor cells will be treated with the various mAb-cytokine chimeras.
relationships: Task 3.3 depends on the produced fusion proteins from Task 3.2 for in vitro validation.
Objective 3 builds upon results from objectives 1 and 2 to design and validate immunocytokines.
